Home»Sport»Confirmed: Time South Africa will be in Olympics 4x100m relay final

Here is the time when you can catch the South African team race for a medal in the men’s 4x100m relay final on Friday.

Team South Africa automatically progressed through to the Men’s 4x100m relay final after finishing second in their Olympics heat on Thursday, with super star Akana Simbine producing a stunning final leg.

With Benjamin Richardson out injured, the team had to reshuffle the order, taking starter Shaun Maswanganyi to the second leg on the back straight, while youngster Bayanda Walaza started with Bradley Nkoana running the bend.

However, it was Simbine – in his usual anchor role – who shot past several competitors in the final straight to to finish second in 37.94 sec to secure a spot in Friday’s final.

The Olympics 4x100m relay final will take place around 19:45 on Friday night.

For the latest Paris Olympics 2024 news, bookmarkThe South African website’sdedicated section for free-to-read content

Team SAare involved in four sports at the 2024 Paris Olympics on Thursday. There’s women’s golf, diving, sport climbing and athletics on the programme.

The United States (27 golds) lead China (also 25 golds), Australia (18), France (13) and Great Britain in the medal standings. Team SA are 37th with one gold (Tatjana Smith), one silver (Smith) and two bronzes (men’s Sevens rugby and Alan Hatherly).

COMPLETED

ATHLETICS

Women’s 100m hurdles, round 1:Marion Fourie (pictured) finished 4th in her heat in 12.91 to qualify 21st overall and she advances to the repechage stage

Women’s javelin qualification, Group A:Jo-Ane van Dyk qualified automatically for the final with her opening throw of 64.22m, which was all she needed to top the group

Men’s 800m, heats:Edmund du Plessis finished 2nd in his heat in 1:45.73 to comfortably qualify for the semi-finals

Men’s high jump qualification, Group B:Brian Raats produced a best jump of 2.24m, at his first attempt at the height, and he qualified for the final

Men’s 200m, semi-finals:Shaun Maswanganyi finished 4th in his semi-final in 20.42 and missed out on a place in the final

Men’s 200m, semi-finals:Wayde van Niekerk finished 7th in his semi-final in 20.72 and missed out on a place in the final

Women’s 400m, semi-finals:Miranda Coetzee finished 8th in her semi-final and missed out on a place in the final

CANOEING

Women’s kayak, single 500m heats:Esti Olivier finished 5th in her heat in 1:55.98 and progressed to the quarter-finals

Women’s kayak, single 500m quarter-final:Esti Olivier finished 6th in her heat in 1:53.21 and did not progress to the semi-finals

Women’s kayak, single 500m heats:Tiffany Koch finished 6th in 2:02.76 and qualified for the quarter-finals

Women’s kayak, single 500m quarter-final:Tiffany Koch finished 6th in her heat in 1:56.81 and did not progress to the semi-finals

Men’s kayak, single 1000m heats:Hamish Lovemore finished 3rd in his heat and qualified for the quarter-finals

Men’s kayak, single 1000m heats:Andrew Birkett finished 5th in his heat and qualified for the quarter-finals

Men’s kayak, single 1000m quarter-finals:Hamish Lovemore finished 2nd in his heat in 3:36.64 and qualified for the semi-finals. Andrew Burkett was 3rd in the same heat in 3:28.11 and was unable to progress to the semi-finals

CYCLING

Men’s sprint, qualifying:Jean Spies finished 29th in qualifying with a time of 9.962 (av speed 72.275 km/h) and didn’t progress to the 1/32 finals

GOLF

Women’s individual, first round:Ashleigh Buhai is sitting in 2nd position after firing an opening round of 68, four-under-par. Paula Reto carded a six-over round of 78

DIVING

Women’s 3m springboard prelims:Julia Vincent qualified for the semi-finals of the competition with a combined score of 283.50. She finished 12th and goes through as one of the top 18

SKATEBOARDING

Men’s Park, prelims:Dallas Oberholzer had a best score of 33.83, which saw him finish 23rd overall and he did not progress to the semi-finals

SPORT CLIMBING

Men’s boulder & Lead, semi-final Lead:Mel Janse van Rensburg added 7.1 Lead points to his boulder points 9.4 for a total of 16.5. He finished 20th and did not progress to the final

description: Here is the time when you can catch the South African team race for a medal in the men's 4x100m relay final on Friday.
